title: CELL STORAGE-01-Freezing and Thawing Protocol for Adherent Cell Lines
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7p8ca","text":"In our institute, cell lines are stored after freezing procedure that provides for a constant temperature lowering by 1 degree/minute. This procedure is useful to minimizes ice crystals formation during freezing process, this allow to reduce the cells damage and, in turn increasing cells viability after thawing. After storage at -80°C or in liquid nitrogen (in relation to subsequent applications or the expected freezing time) cells are thawed rapidly in a water bath at 37°C and the cryopreserving-medium is immediately remove by gentle centrifugation in order to reduce as soon as possible, the DMSO toxic effect.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d6isq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t1ln","text":"Currently, in our institute, the freezing/thawing procedure is validated for the following cell steps: Add 4 mL of complete medium to inactivate trypsin, recover the cell suspension in 15 mL conical tube and centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 200 x g, °C, Room temperature .
At confluence remove culture medium from the flasks, wash twice the cell monolayer with 5 mL of sterile PBS.
Add 1 mL (for T25 cm 2 culture flasks) of trypsin/EDTA for 5-10 minutes, observe the cells under a microscope to make sure that cells are detached.
Remove supernatants and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of cryopreserving medium (90% FBS + 10% DMSO).
Immediately aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension into a cryogenic vial.
Place the cryogenic vial(s) in cotton wool, and then place it in a polystyrene box.
Put the polystyrene box in -80°C freezer. -80°C Freezer (Eppendorf, B U9230-0001)
After a minimum of 24:00:00 transfer the cryogenic vials into a box for storage in -80°C freezer.
Remove cryogenic vial from -80°C and keep it at 37 °C in water bath, this procedure should take approximately 1-2 minutes or until ampoule is slightly thawed.
Clean the cryogenic vial outside with 70% EtOH, open the cryogenic vial and add 1 mL of prewarmed complete medium for specific cells lines (Table 2) .
Transfer the cell suspension into a 15 mL conical tube containing 10 mL of prewarmed complete medium for specific cells lines.
Centrifuge for 00:05:00 at 200 x g, °C, at Room temperature
Resuspended the cells into appropriate cell culture medium ( Table 2 ) and start quality control procedure ( Table 1 ).
creator: Farmacologia Medica
